subject: Meeting with Brathwait and Oriole
body:
John,
 
I need to make this very clear.  No one, including you as our local  CZM 
attorney, is to even ask for a meeting with CZM before clearing it through  JEE 
(i.e., me).  Asking for a meeting and canceling if JEE does not approve  is not 
the same thing and is equally inappropriate.  Also, two  professionals must 
attend any CZM meetings on JEE's behalf, and one of them must  be me, unless JEE 
determines otherwise.  These rules are absolute and must  be followed to a 
tee.    
 
I understand from you that you have already asked for a meeting with  
Brathwaite and JP Oriol regarding both the release for the violation and the  
procedures required to clean up the necessary water approvals (ACOE and CZM) for  the 
dock, rip rap, intake line, bulkhead, etc..  I understand from you that  CZM 
has called back to schedule the meeting tomorrow and although you may cancel  
the meeting by calling tomorrow morning, if you do, you could very likely  
lose the ability to work with the same personnel on the CZM side who originally  
advised you on the process for finalizing all the approvals necessary for the  
dock.  Based on what you have told me, you should go to the meeting,  but:
 
1.    Bring Monique to take detailed notes and prepare  minutes (for our 
internal purposes only and not to be offered to or shared with  CZM without 
JEE/DKI approval) of what transpires at the meeting.
 
2.    Do not volunteer any information about LSJ or  suggest procedures about 
how to clean up and finalize things.  ASK what  they want step by step so 
that we can fully comply because what we want to do is  make sure we comply with 
their requirements.  
 
If they want something more than they originally promised you, do not  argue. 
 Report that back to me and we will discuss as a group how to  address the 
inconsistency.  If something is unclear then ask for  clarification, but do not 
volunteer your own interpretation and don't assume you  know what they intend. 
 Have them clarify if possible.
 
Please make sure that Monique is prepared to take very careful notes.
 
Thanks.
 
Darren K.  Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison Avenue
4th Floor
New York, New  York 10022
Tel: 212-750-1176
Fax: 212-750-0381
e-mail: _dkiesq@aol.com_ (mailto:dkiesq@aol.com) 

------------------------------

 or marketing by other parties of the transaction(s) or matter(s) addressed 
in  this email. Each taxpayer should seek advice based on the taxpayer's 
particular  circumstances from an independent tax advisor.

body_html:
John,
 
I need to make this very clear.  No one, including you as our local CZM attorney, is to even ask for a meeting with CZM before clearing it through JEE (i.e., me).  Asking for a meeting and canceling if JEE does not approve is not the same thing and is equally inappropriate.  Also, two professionals must attend any CZM meetings on JEE's behalf, and one of them must be me, unless JEE determines otherwise.  These rules are absolute and must be followed to a tee.   
 
I understand from you that you have already asked for a meeting with Brathwaite and JP Oriol regarding both the release for the violation and the procedures required to clean up the necessary water approvals (ACOE and CZM) for the dock, rip rap, intake line, bulkhead, etc..  I understand from you that CZM has called back to schedule the meeting tomorrow and although you may cancel the meeting by calling tomorrow morning, if you do, you could very likely lose the ability to work with the same personnel on the CZM side who originally advised you on the process for finalizing all the approvals necessary for the dock.  Based on what you have told me, you should go to the meeting, but:
 
1.    Bring Monique to take detailed notes and prepare minutes (for our internal purposes only and not to be offered to or shared with CZM without JEE/DKI approval) of what transpires at the meeting.
 
2.    Do not volunteer any information about LSJ or suggest procedures about how to clean up and finalize things.  ASK what they want step by step so that we can fully comply because what we want to do is make sure we comply with their requirements.  
 
If they want something more than they originally promised you, do not argue.  Report that back to me and we will discuss as a group how to address the inconsistency.  If something is unclear then ask for clarification, but do not volunteer your own interpretation and don't assume you know what they intend.  Have them clarify if possible.
 
Please make sure that Monique is prepared to take very careful notes.
 
Thanks.
 
Darren K. Indyke
